data.title: South Korea's Official Foreign Reserves Marginally Decline in May
data.description:
South Korea's foreign reserves marginally contracted to $404.6 billion in May from $404.7 billion as of end-April, the Bank of Korea said Thursday.

The country's official forex reserves consisted of securities amounting to $360 billion, deposits of $19.7 billion, special drawing rights of $15.7 billion, gold worth $4.8 billion, and the country's IMF position at $4.5 billion.

South Korea's forex reserve holdings ranked tenth largest globally as of the end of April, the central bank said.

data.title: South Korea's GDP Declines by 0.2% in Q1 2025 from Q4 2024
data.description:
South Korea's real gross domestic product decreased 0.2% in the first quarter of 2025 from the previous quarter, according to an advance estimate by the Bank of Korea on Thursday.

On an annual basis, real GDP remained unchanged.

Meanwhile, real gross national income increased by 0.1% on an on-quarter basis in Q1 and rose 0.7% on an on-year basis, the release said.

data.title: Samil C&S Wins 13.4 Billion Won Order
data.description:
Samil C&S Co Ltd :

  • SAMIL C&S: WINS 13.4 BILLION WON ORDER

data.title: Philippine Central Bank Could Cut Rates in August, 4Q — Market Talk
data.description: The Philippine central bank could hold rates in June and ease in August and 4Q, Barclays economist Shreya Sodhani writes in a note. BSP Gov. Eli Remolona recently noted that the central bank could cut rates twice before the year ends, but not necessarily consecutively. There are downside risks to growth and inflation, suggesting that more rate cuts are needed, Sodhani notes. However, she says that the Federal Reserve's path should play an equally important role in determining the magnitude of rate cuts by the BSP. Barclays U.S. economists expect the Fed to deliver one more cut this year, in December. "This gives the BSP further reason to stay on hold when it announces its decisions half a day after the Fed's June meeting," Sodhani says. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Unite Group Unit to Sell Nine UK Properties in GBP212 Million Deal
data.description:
Unite Group said Thursday that Unite Students agreed to sell nine UK properties to a Lone Star Funds affiliate.

The student accommodation company will divest the locations for 212 million pounds sterling. The sale is part of its effort to boost its long-term rental growth prospects.

The portfolio comprises a total of 3,656 beds in Aberdeen, Leeds, Leicester, Nottingham, and Sheffield.

data.title: Evonik’s methionine sites in Singapore and Antwerp to undergo planned turnaround
data.description:
Evonik Industries AG said it will continue to optimize its global methionine production setup for its DL-methionine in the third and fourth quarter of 2025, as per Chemweek.

It said implementation of several technical measures requires a shutdown of the production facilities for methionine and its precursors for 6-7 weeks each at Evonik's Jurong Island, Singapore site in August/September and at its Antwerp, Belgium, site in September/October 2025.In Singapore, the maintenance schedule of a raw material supplier necessitates an early shutdown in third quarter. Evonik will synchronize this with the catalyst exchange in its methionine production and will improve the energy efficiency and CO2 emissions of the plant.In Antwerp, Evonik will also combine a catalyst exchange with the continuation of the measures that begun in May to reduce nitrogen, sulphur and CO2 emissions. During the shutdown of the methionine facilities for 6-7 weeks in September and October, the Evonik Antwerp site will implement further milestones from its climate roadmap and make the site’s utilities infrastructure fit for the future.The company last year completed an expansion of its DL-methionine plant at Jurong Island.Methionine is an essential amino acid used as an animal feed supplement.

mrchub.com

data.title: REG - TempletonEmerg.Mkt. - Net Asset Value(s)
data.description:
RNS Number : 6002L Templeton Emerging Markets IT PLC 05 June 2025  Templeton Emerging Markets Investment Trust PLC ('TEMIT')         On behalf of TEMIT, Franklin Templeton Investment Trust Management Limited reports the unaudited cum-income net asset value ('NAV') of TEMIT as at     04 June 2025 was  £2034.471m, representing a NAV of 200.90 pence per share. The unaudited ex-income NAV of TEMIT as at 04 June 2025 was  £1982.560m, representing a NAV of 195.77 pence per share.     In accordance with the Association of Investment Companies recommendations, the cum-income NAV has been calculated based on the total value of underlying assets,    including accumulated or accrued income, and using bid price information relating to the underlying assets.    The ex-income NAV has been calculated based on the total value of underlying assets, excluding these income elements, and using bid price information relating to the underlying assets.         Portfolio data for TEMIT can be found on the website www.TEMIT.co.uk.         For information please contact Client Dealer Services on freephone 0800 305 306.    This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

data.title: REG-Early Closure of Subscription Process for 3rd Tranche of UAB "Kvartalas" Bonds
data.description:
UAB “Kvartalas”, legal entity code 305475438, with its registered address at Jogailos St. 4, Vilnius, Republic of Lithuania (the Issuer), announces that, due to sufficient investor demand, the Issuer has decided to end the subscription process for the offering of the 3rd tranche of bonds (ISIN LT0000411167), effective as of the time of this announcement. New subscription orders are no longer accepted.

As of the time of this announcement, Nasdaq Exchange Members shall not accept any new subscription orders from investors. By 12:00 midday today (5 June 2025) the Members may only enter into the Exchange Trading System previously received buy orders that have not yet been entered. Any orders entered into the Exchange Trading System after 12:00 will not be processed and will not be eligible for bond allocation.

Please note that the results of the offering of the 3rd tranche of bonds will be announced publicly in a separate notice.

On behalf of the Issuer:

Ieva Antanaitytė-Genevičienė

General Manager

ieva.geneviciene@lordslb.lt

data.title: Man Industries (India) Bags Export Order Worth INR11.5 Billion
data.description:
Man Industries (India) received a new export order worth 11.50 billion Indian rupees from an undisclosed international client, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The contract is for the supply of various types of pipes to be delivered during the next six to 12 months. With this, the company's order book has increased to around 35 billion rupees.

The company's shares were up over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: German Factory Demand Points to Continued Turnaround — Market Talk
data.description:
0748 GMT - A recovery in German industry remains underway, ING macro analyst Carsten Brzeski says, after orders to the country's factories unexpectedly rose at the start of the second quarter. Rising order books over the previous three months had been pinned to a rush from U.S. importers to stock up ahead of tariffs, but that now looks only part of the story. Foreign orders retreated only slightly in April and domestic demand proved robust. "The feared full reversal of the frontloading effect of the first quarter has not materialised," Brzeski says. "Instead, it looks as if the turning of the German industrial cycle is continuing." (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0742 GMT - Yields on U.K. government bonds, or gilts, fall, tracking declines in their U.S. and eurozone equivalents. It follows Wednesday's weaker-than-expected U.S. labor market data and ISM services index. These data raised confidence that the Fed would still cut interest rates this year, Deutsche Bank Research strategists say in a note. Investors also await a European Central Bank rate decision due at 1215 GMT, where an interest rate cut is widely expected. The 10-year gilt yields fall 2 basis points to 4.600%, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0735 GMT - South Korea's new Democratic Party government could face challenges in tariff talks with the U.S. if the Trump administration seeks to weaken Korean-Chinese trade linkages, Fitch Ratings analysts write in a note. The Democratic Party has historically sought balance in the country's ties with the U.S. and China, they say. They also see a lot of uncertainty over U.S. trade policy. South Korea is subject to a 25% reciprocal tariff that Trump has proposed but temporarily paused for negotiations. The U.S. may impose new levies on semiconductors, a major South Korean export item, in addition to current duties of 25% on autos and 50% on steel, Fitch adds. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0729 GMT - If the European Central Bank reduces its inflation forecasts significantly when it announces a policy decision, it will likely limit the euro's gains against a weaker dollar, XTB's Kathleen Brooks says in a note. The ECB is widely expected to cut interest rates by 25 basis points, taking the deposit rate to 2.0%. Recent weak eurozone inflation data mean inflation forecasts are also expected to be reduced. "A large downward revision to the ECB's inflation forecasts could limit the euro's upside against the dollar in the short term," Brooks says. The euro has gained ground against the dollar over the past month, despite European bond yields lagging the U.S., she says. The euro rises 0.1% to $1.1423. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0715 GMT - The U.S. dollar edges slightly higher against a basket of currencies in early European trade but remains weak and close to six-week lows. The dollar fell on Wednesday after weak U.S. ADP private payrolls data, while trade-policy uncertainty also weighs on the U.S. currency. "Interestingly, the U.S. dollar is one of the few assets reflecting trade pessimism," says Swissquote Bank's Ipek Ozkardeskaya in a note. The DXY index rises 0.1% to 98.849, having hit a six-week low of 98.583 earlier this week. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0702 GMT - A deceleration in Philippines' headline inflation to 1.3% in May from 1.4% the previous month raises the risk of a policy rate cut this month, says Aris Dacanay of HSBC Global Research. The peso's relative strength could also give Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas room to lower policy rates, the economist says in a note. That said, the June 19 meeting remains a tough call as upside risks to inflation remain. Dacanay points to the lower house of Congress's approval of an across-the-board minimum-wage hike this week. Policymakers are also considering raising tariff rates on rice. If this materializes, BSP may need to reconsider the pace of its easing cycle, he adds. HSBC's baseline scenario is for the central bank to pause its easing cycle in June. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0658 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly lower. IG calls the OMXS30 down 0.1% at around 2508. Major stock exchanges were somewhat mixed on Wednesday, SEB analysts say in a note. Weak U.S. economic data increased expectations that the Federal Reserve will cut its policy rate in the coming months. Stock markets in Asia have moved in slightly different directions overnight and futures are close to zero in both the U.S. and Europe. ECB policymakers are expected to lower on Thursday and SEB doesn't expect the central bank to commit to a given rate path ahead. OMXS30 closed at 2510.47, OMXN40 at 2413.70 and OBX at 1499.55. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0616 GMT - France's 10 billion euro-12 billion euro issuance of long-dated government bonds on Thursday looks set to weigh on Bunds in the earlier part of the session, Commerzbank Research's Hauke Siemssen says in a note. However, this potential rise in Bund yields could be used for tactical long positions ahead of the European Central Bank's meeting, the rates strategist says. Money markets fully price a 25-basis-point rate cut by the ECB on Thursday, bringing the deposit rate to 2.00%. Significant falls in U.S. Treasury yields on Wednesday following weak data left German Bunds unfazed. The 10-year Bund yield is flat at 2.521% shortly after market open, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0610 GMT - U.S. Treasury yields are stabilizing after sharp declines Wednesday prompted by weak data. The two-, 10- and 30-year Treasury yields are trading flat at 3.879%, 4.367% and 4.890%, respectively, according to Tradeweb. Wednesday's declines of 8 basis points in the two-year yield and 9.5 basis points in the 10-year yield were prompted among others by a below-consensus ADP jobs report, which puts Friday's nonfarm payrolls data under even bigger scrutiny than usual. However, further signs of weakness in the labor-market data are needed before the Federal Reserve can cut interest rates, Metzler analysts Leon Ferdinand Bost and Uwe Hohmann say in a note. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0537 GMT - The European Central Bank is expected to give no clear signal for July, with risks leaning toward signaling a pause, says Pictet Wealth Management's Nadia Gharbi in a note. "Importantly, by the July meeting, several key developments will help clarify the policy debate," the senior economist says. These include the conclusion of the 90-day tariff pause, possibly more information on the German budgets for 2025 and 2026, two additional PMI reports scheduled for June 23 and July 24, and one more inflation report due July 1, she says. Pictet WM expects the ECB to lower its growth and inflation forecasts for 2025 and 2026 and conclude that monetary policy easing is warranted. Pictet WM expects a 25-basis-point rate cut today. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0532 GMT - Ongoing declines in eurozone inflation and consistently dovish language from European Central Bank members make a rate cut appear a done deal on Thursday, says Lazard Asset Management's Ronald Temple in a note. The ECB has previously described 1.75%-2.25% as the range that would be considered neutral monetary policy--one that neither stimulates nor cools the economy. "Any signals of a change in this view would be surprising," the chief market strategist says. "I continue to expect rates to be reduced to 1.5% by year end given a more aggressive U.S. trade posture against the European Union." His forecast is marginally below money market expectations for rates to end the year around 1.70%, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0513 GMT - The Reserve Bank of India is likely to cut its policy repo rate by 25 bps to 5.75% on Friday, Commerzbank Research analysts say in a research report. India's central bank downgraded its growth forecast to 6.5% at April's monetary-policy meeting, citing "exceptional uncertainties," the analysts note. Also, India's inflation is subdued, with headline inflation in April at 3.2% on-year, below RBI's 4% midpoint target. This gives the RBI room to cut rates to "cushion the downside risks to growth," the analysts add. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Chinese Stocks Continue with Rally Amid Increased Service Activity in May; Two Debutants Close Higher
data.description:
Chinese stocks continued their upward trend on Thursday after an indicator of service activity rose for the 29th straight time in May.

The Shanghai Composite Index, the main gauge of Chinese stocks, rose 0.2% or by 7.90 points to 3,384.10. The Shenzhen Component Index increased by 0.6% or by 58.92 points to 10,203.50.

China's services sector grew faster in May as the Caixin China General Services Business Activity Index accelerated to 51.1 in May from 50.7 in April despite a decline in new export orders amid tariff threats.

Meanwhile, power rates in China's major industrial hubs also fell due to a decline in the country's coal prices and a deluge of green energy, Bloomberg News reported separately Thursday.

The lower power prices helped alleviate pressures on factories hurt by the US-China trade war, the report said.

In the corporate front, debutant Shenzhen UUGreenPower closed 69% higher during its first trading day in Shenzhen. The new energy vehicle charging equipment component manufacturer opened 67% higher at 150.01 yuan early Thursday.

Another debutant, Zhongce Rubber , closed 7% higher. The rubber tire manufacturer priced its initial public offering at 46.50 yuan apiece.

data.title: Breton Technology's Stake in Unit Drops as Controlling Shareholder Injects Capital
data.description:
Breton Technology's controlling shareholder Shanghai Yijin Investment agreed to inject 20 million yuan of additional registered capital in Baipin (Shanghai) Intelligent Technology, a unit of the company, a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The capital increases will raise Baipin (Shanghai) Intelligent Technology's registered capital to 60 million yuan from 40 million yuan as a result.

Following completion, electric-powered engineering machinery company Breton Technology's shareholding in the unit will fall to around 66.67%.

data.title: U.S. Futures Flat, European Stocks Edge Up
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were flat and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average were flat. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 gained 0.1% in morning trading. Wise rose 8.7% and Vestas Wind Systems climbed 4.3%. On the other hand, Hemnet Group dropped 6.7%, and Avolta sunk 5.3%. The FTSE 100 added 0.1%. Other stocks in Europe were up as France's CAC 40 increased 0.2% and Germany's DAX climbed 0.1%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index was flat at 95.21.

In commodities, Brent crude gained 0.4% to $65.13 a barrel, and WTI crude climbed 0.3% to $63.04 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 1.5% to 36.2 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield held steady at 2.525%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury rose by 1 basis point to 4.373% from 4.359%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were mixed as Japan's Nikkei 225 index fell 0.5%, whereas Hong Kong's Hang Seng climbed 0.9%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite climbed 0.2%.

-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: CMC Markets' Earnings Disappoint — Market Talk
data.description: CMC Markets' full-year earnings were disappointing, Shore Capital analyst Vivek Raja writes. The London-listed online trading platform's 84.5-million-pound pretax profit missed the 92.1 million pounds Shore Capital had expected. Costs were also disappointing, he adds. Operating costs excluding variable remuneration of 230.2 million pounds is higher than guidance of around 225 million pounds, he writes. Based on the earnings, he expects modest mid-single digit earnings and dividend per share reductions. Having performed well ahead of the results, CMC's share price should weaken on the back of the statement, he adds. Shares trade down 8.8% at 259.5 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: Italian Shares Fall Ahead of ECB Decision
data.description:
The Italian stock market fell to around 39,990 on Thursday, continuing to trade within a tight range as investors remained cautious ahead of the European Central Bank’s monetary policy decision later in the day.

The central bank is widely expected to cut interest rates again, potentially followed by a pause to assess the impact of US trade policies on the eurozone economy.

Domestically, traders digested fresh PMI data indicating a continued but modest expansion in the construction sector.

Among early laggards were Snam (-1%), Stellantis (-0.7%), and Italgas (-0.9%).

On the upside, Buzzi Unicem (+1.3%), Interpump Group (+1%), and Leonardo (+0.7%) led early gains.

data.title: TotalEnergies begins phase 2 of Absheron field development in Azerbaijan
data.description:
TotalEnergies , a participants in the project to develop the Absheron gas condensate field in Azerbaijan's sector of the Caspian Sea, has begun implementing the second phase of the project, the head of the French company's division in Azerbaijan, Emmanuel de Guillebon said at a Baku energy forum, as per Interfax.He said the concept for the project calls for underwater production with the drilling of three or four additional wells and construction of a 140-km pipeline to the terminal in Sangachal.There are plans to build a new gas processing plant near the terminal, he said. A key element of the project will be a multiphase pipeline that will carry gas and condensate together, which makes it possible to use pipe with lower heat insulation and essentially cut construction costs by half, Guillebon said.The director of asset management at XRG, Bassem Tadros said at the forum that this project is just the beginning, as discussions are underway on new projects with SOCAR and other partners and progress is expected in the coming months. XRG is an international investment arm of United Arab Emirates state oil and gas company ADNOC, which also has a stake in the Absheron project.The company is considering increasing investment in Azerbaijan, which it sees not only has a country with a wealth of oil and gas resources, but also one that is ideally suited for developing renewable energy sources, from geothermal energy to biomass, Tadros said.The president of State Oil Company of Azerbaijan Republic (SOCAR), Rovshan Najaf met in Baku with Tadros and TotalEnergies senior vice president for Europe Jean-Luc Guiziou."We discussed our ongoing joint projects, particularly the full-scale development of the Absheron gas-condensate field. We also exchanged views on renewable energy, decarbonization, innovative technologies, and other issues of mutual interest," Najaf posted on Linkedin.It was reported earlier that the second phase of Absheron's development will at least double gas production at the field to 3 billion cubic meters per year from the 1.5 bcm being produced in the first phase. Gas produced under the first phase is intended for Azerbaijan's domestic market.TotalEnergies and SOCAR announced the start of production under the first phase of the Absheron field's development in July 2023. First-phase production peaked at 1.5 bcm in 2024. It was previously expected that production under the project could peak at 5.5 bcm-6 bcm per year.The Absheron project was initially equally owned by SOCAR and TotalEnergies, but in August 2023 they each agreed to sell a 15% stake to Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC), giving the latter a 30% stake and reducing their interests to 35% each.The Absheron block is located 100 km from Baku at a depth of 500 meters, with the contract area covering 747 square km. The initial estimate put the field's reserves at 350 bcm of gas and 100 million tonnes of condensate. The field is being developed in stages under a 30-year contract by the Joint Operating Company of Absheron Petroleum B.V. (JOCAP).

mrchub.com

data.title: Praram 9 Hospital's Earnings Momentum Likely to Stay Strong — Market Talk
data.description: Praram 9 Hospital's earnings momentum is likely to stay strong in 2Q-3Q, says Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Nontapat Sahakitpinyo. The management has highlighted the hospital operator's revenue for 2Q-to-date grew on yearly and quarterly basis, aided by a rebound in Middle East patients, the analyst notes. The Thailand hospital operator's revenue is also expected to grow further in 3Q, driven by high seasonal demand and continued growth from the Middle East. The brokerage lifts its earnings forecasts for Praram 9 Hospital by 8% for 2025, 5% for 2026, and 3% for 2027. It raises the stock's target price to THB32.50 from THB32.00, with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 0.4% lower at THB24.30. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Toyo Denki Seizo Books 281 Million Yen Gain from Sale of Cross-Shareholdings
data.description:
Toyo Denki Seizo has booked an extraordinary income of 281 million yen from the sale of a portion of its cross-shareholdings, according to its bourse filing on Wednesday.

The sale, executed between August 2024 and May 2025, involved four listed and one unlisted security.

This gain has already been incorporated into the company's consolidated earnings forecast for the fiscal year ending May 31.

data.title: Tiancheng Controls Gets Order to Produce Vehicle Seats For Automobile Maker
data.description:
Zhejiang Tiancheng Controls received an order to supply passenger vehicle seats to a domestic automobile manufacturer, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Thursday.

The Chinese vehicle seat maker will make the seats for the client's two projects, starting in September and October, respectively. One will run for two years and the other will last for five years.

About 215,800 vehicles are estimated to be produced during the projects' period, the disclosure said.

data.title: Australia shares pause after recent gains
data.description:
Key points:
  • ASX200 takes a pause after recent gains
  • CBA retreats from record high
  • Miners log third straight session of gains

Australian shares ended flat on Thursday as investors took a breather amid a rally sparked by heightened interest rate cut hopes and waited for fresh catalysts to guide the market forward.

The S&P/ASX 200 index shed a marginal 0.03% to end at 8,538.90 points.

The benchmark posted monthly gains in April and May, rising 16.3% since the April 7 slump triggered by U.S. President Donald Trump's "reciprocal tariffs".

A range of indicators aided the recovery - the Reserve Bank of Australia's (RBA) meeting minutes from May revealing consideration of an outsized rate cut and the recent GDP print showing barely any growth in the first quarter.

Investors will be closely watching two key events this week to gauge market direction: the scheduled talks between U.S President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping and the U.S. employment data due on Friday.

Expectations of central bank rate cuts are fuelling calls for markets to scale new heights, Market strategist at moomoo, Jessica Amir, said, while noting minor profit-taking in key sectors.

The financial sub-index eased 0.1% after its biggest component by weight, Commonwealth Bank of Australia , receded from Wednesday's record high. Of the remaining of its "Big Four" peers, only Westpac gained, rising 0.5%.

Bucking the trend, miners added 0.6%, led by rare-earth miner Lynas hitting a more than two-year high after global automakers flagged production delays, citing China's restriction on rare earth exports.

BHP's 0.1% rise boosted the sub-index.

Technology stocks advanced 0.7%, pushed higher by data centre operator NextDC's 0.4% climb.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index finished the session 0.7% higher at 12,577.15 points.

data.title: Eurozone Construction Downturn Deepens
data.description:
The HCOB Eurozone Construction PMI fell to 45.6 in May 2025 from 46 in April, signaling a deeper contraction in construction activity across the euro area.

Both total activity and new order inflows declined at similarly sharp rates, prompting further reductions in employment and purchasing activity.

Across sectors, housing activity remained the worst-performing segment, though falls in commercial and civil engineering activity were also relatively sharp.

Regionally, France and Germany were the main drags, both posting steeper contractions, while Italy stood out as the lone bright spot, managing a third straight month of growth.

Regarding prices, input cost inflation accelerated to a 17-month high.

Despite these challenges, eurozone construction firms expressed optimism about the outlook for activity over the next 12 months—marking the first instance of positive sentiment since February 2022.

data.title: London copper inches higher on weaker dollar; tariff developments in focus
data.description:
London copper prices inched up on Thursday amid a weakening U.S. dollar, while the market focus remained on a series of trade talks between the U.S. and its major trading partners.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange gained nearly 0.4% to $9,656.5 per metric ton, as of 0702 GMT, and the most-traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange held steady at 78,170 yuan ($10,884.46) per ton.

The dollar index , which measures the U.S. currency against six others, was at 98.87 and has dropped about 9% this year, poised for its weakest yearly performance since 2017.

A softer U.S. dollar makes dollar-denominated assets more affordable to holders of other currencies.

On the trade front, U.S. President Donald Trump called his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping tough and "extremely hard to make a deal with" on Wednesday, exposing frictions after the White House raised expectations of a long-awaited phone call between the two leaders this week.

Canada prepared possible reprisals, while the European Union reported progress in trade talks on Wednesday, as new U.S. metals tariffs triggered more disruption in the global economy and added urgency to negotiations with Washington.

"Trump's 50% tariffs on aluminium and steel have raised expectations that he will soon follow through with a pledge to impose stiffer duties on copper as well," ANZ said.

Tin on the LME eased around 0.2% to $31,950 a ton after hitting a one-week high on Wednesday on concerns that the resumption of supply from Myanmar's tin-rich Wa State would take longer than previously expected.

Among other London metals, LME aluminium ticked down 0.2% to $2,478 a ton, lead eased 0.4% to $1,983, while nickel edged up 0.3% to $15,440.

SHFE tin remained the strongest performer on Thursday, up 1.5% to 258,900 yuan, aluminium stayed steady at 20,010 yuan a ton, lead added 0.4% to 16,695 yuan, while nickel was down 0.4% to 121,570 yuan.

data.title: Flex Insider Sold Shares Worth $2,660,861, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Kwang Hooi Tan, Chief Operating Officer, on June 02, 2025, sold 62,292 shares in Flex for $2,660,861. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Tan has control over a total of 269,151 ordinary shares of the company, with 269,151 shares held directly.

SEC Filing:

data.title: Wizz Air Shares Tumble After Earnings Decline on Higher Costs
data.description:
By Pierre Bertrand

Shares in Wizz Air tumbled in early European trading after the company posted a decline in net profit as operating expenses rose.

The low-cost airline reported 213.9 million euros ($244.3 million) in net profit for the year to the end of March, compared with 365.9 million euros a year prior, on revenue which grew 3.8% to 5.27 billion and reflected better pricing.

The airline said that though it flew a record 63.4 million passengers, which supported increases in ancillary and ticket revenue, total unit cost rose nearly 11% and operating expenses excluding fuel increased about 19% to 3.3 billion euros.

Operating expenses including fuel rose 10% to 5.1 billion euros, the company said.

Shares in the low-cost airline fell 23% at 12.90 pounds.

"It was a year of significant challenges given an average of 44 aircraft were parked during the year owing to issues with the GTF engine, equivalent to almost 20% of the fleet being grounded," the company said Thursday, adding the grounding of aircrafts contributed to higher costs.

The company is expecting roughly 34 aircraft to be grounded by the end of the first half of fiscal 2026.

Wizz Air didn't disclose guidance for fiscal 2026, citing lack of visibility.

That said, summer trading so far was showing signs of positive revenue from each plane seat available for sale, but fares were down by low single-digits to drive traffic, Wizz Air said.

It added that current bookings were supportive of the company posting higher revenue in fiscal 2026, but said that it forecasts slightly higher costs per available plane seat flown excluding fuel.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: Tosei Reports No Share Repurchase Activity in May
data.description:
Tosei did not repurchase any shares between May 1 and May 31 under its ongoing buyback program, the company said Thursday.

The plan, authorized on April 7, allows for the purchase of up to 700,000 shares or 1 billion yen through Nov. 30, the filing said.

As of May 31, no shares have been repurchased under the authorization, the company said.

data.title: French Construction Sector Downturn Intensifies
data.description:
The HCOB Construction PMI in France fell to 43.1 in May 2025, from 43.6 in the previous month, marking another sharp monthly contraction and extending the sector's downturn into its third consecutive year.

The latest figure also represented the steepest contraction since February, with the housing sector continuing to be the main source of weakness, posting another significant drop in building activity.

New orders have now been falling steadily since April 2022, with survey respondents citing slow decision-making by clients as a key factor hampering activity.

In addition, construction firms continued to reduce staffing levels, although the pace of job cuts was only marginal and the slowest in nine months.

On the pricing front, input costs rose to a three-month high—though overall inflation remained historically subdued.

Looking ahead, French constructors became more pessimistic about the twelve-month outlook, with firms citing weak demand as the primary concern.

data.title: Ignitis Starts Operations at Kelmė Wind Farm II in Lithuania
data.description:
AB Ignitis grupė (, IGN1L.VS) said Thursday its subsidiary Ignitis Renewables launched commercial operations at Kelmė wind farm II, Lithuania.

The Lithuanian energy group said the 199.6 megawatt facility consists of 28 Nordex turbines and raised its total green capacity to 1.7 gigawatts.

The total investment in the 313.7 megawatt Kelmė project, comprising wind farms I and II, stood at 550 million euros.
